extraction.one_line_summary: VALGREN Michael won after QUICK-STEP brought two riders to final selection, but their dual presence led to tactical paralysis in final 700m sprint
tactical_patterns: QUICK-STEP two-man advantage (GASPAROTTO, KREUZIGER) in finale froze tactics, allowing VALGREN to win from behind at 300m.
CRADDOCK Lawson survived from early break to finish 9th, showing breakaway remnants viable in selective finale.
Cauberg climb 43.5km out shattered break but was too early for decisive selection among favorites.

rider_signals.VALGREN Michael: positive: Won both Omloop and Amstel showing dual capability in cobbled semi-classics and hilly Ardennes races
rider_signals.GASPAROTTO Enrico: neutral: Strong enough for podium but QUICK-STEP numbers advantage created sprint hesitation costing potential win
rider_signals.KREUZIGER Roman: neutral: Second place masks tactical failure where two QUICK-STEP riders watched each other instead of attacking
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Fourth place marks continued Amstel failure despite presence in winning move, lacks decisive punch on this parcours
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: 9th from early break shows exceptional diesel endurance on repeated climbing circuits
